hq: No physical office (operates from managed working space)

system_description.overview: Bicameral provides AI-powered financial document automation tools for factoring companies and aging/accounts receivable management. The platform automates punchlist and document workflows for financial services clients.

products.Aging Extraction: Automated extraction of aging reports from financial documents
products.Factoring Portfolio Audit: Automated audit tooling for factoring portfolios

infrastructure.cloud_provider: AWS (carve-out — AWS physical/environmental controls excluded from scope)
regions: Not explicitly named; single primary region inferred
infrastructure.physical_presence: None — no office; operates from building-managed working space
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (daily backups with multi-AZ configuration confirmed in CC7.4.2)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: VPC with subnets; system components configured so company and customer access is appropriately segmented from other customer accounts (CC6.1.3). Network segmentation separates systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality (CC6.1.7).
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management. Network firewalls implemented, configured, and managed to control and monitor incoming/outgoing traffic (CC6.6.1, CC6.6.2, CC6.6.3).
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS utilized to monitor and detect unauthorized access or anomalies within the network (CC2.1.1). Firewalls and intrusion detection tools configured to prevent unauthorized access and detect external security threats (CC6.6.1).
network_architecture.vpn: Bastion host for remote access (inferred from standard architecture; MFA enforced for all remote access CC6.1.1)
network_architecture.tls: Encryption technologies used to protect communication and transmission of data over public networks (CC6.1.6). TLS version not specified.
network_architecture.topology: Internet → VPC → AZ → Subnets containing ECR, Lambda, EC2, S3, DynamoDB. Firewalls at application gateway and production network perimeter.

application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments (CC8.1.4). Changes tested in dev/test before promotion to production.

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS IAM (password policy governed by AWS; unique user IDs and passwords required for production environment and infrastructure)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all user accounts with administrative access to production platform (CC6.1.1). MFA enforced for all remote access (CC6.1.1). MFA required to access source code management tool (CC8.1.3).
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Controlled through AWS password policy; includes periodic forced changes, password expiry and complexity requirements. User accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts. Guest/anonymous logins not allowed. Unattended desktops lock on inactivity.

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (AWS KMS)
encryption.key_management: AWS KMS — access to production environment access keys restricted to authorized individuals (CC6.1.5)

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named (generic 'source code management tool' referenced)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to require additional approval for every merge request to move changes to production environment (CC8.1.2). Developers cannot make production changes without additional approval.
ci_cd_devops.iac: Baseline configurations retained within infrastructure as code; configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback capability (CC7.1.1, CC8.1.1).
ci_cd_devops.container_orchestration: ECR referenced (container registry); orchestration layer not named
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO/Management set up automatic patch management. Monthly patch management reviews. Workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned for patch compliance daily (CC7.5.1).

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named (generic 'monitoring tool' generates alerts when predefined thresholds met)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: AWS logging (logging enabled for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions, function/config/permission/role changes at application and infrastructure level)
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events. Tracked until resolved within change-management/ticketing application.

security_tools.antivirus: Endpoint Security Solution installed on all desktops and laptops within scope. Anti-virus software with automatic scanning; virus definitions updated daily from vendor website. Anti-virus and Malware protection on production servers updated weekly and on Mac workstations/laptops.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly — automated tool performs vulnerability assessment on infrastructure and applications; scan frequency adjustable. Management reviews and remediates high/critical findings.
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party. High/critical findings tracked and remediated.

untestable: CC2.2.3 — No significant changes to people/roles/responsibilities during engagement period
CC3.2.1 (cybersecurity insurance) — No cyber incidents reported during engagement period
CC6.5.2 — No customer terminations during engagement period
CC7.3.1 — No security incidents reported during engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical security of data center is responsibility of AWS (subservice org)
A1.2 — Environmental security controls managed by building management/AWS; Bicameral has no office
notable_controls: Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) meets semiannually; IT Leadership Committee meets weekly
Board of Directors has independent members; meets annually for governance review
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
IaC baseline configurations with rollback capability
Emergency change process with accelerated approval timeline
Vendor management with quarterly risk assessments for vendors without SOC reports

infrastructure.rationale: AWS multi-AZ with Lambda, EC2, ECR, S3, DynamoDB. IaC confirmed. Single region inferred (no multi-region stated). No CDN mentioned. Solid mid-tier AWS deployment.

application_architecture.rationale: Hybrid Lambda + EC2 architecture with containerization (ECR). Separate environments, branch protection, code review enforced. Languages/frameworks not disclosed. Limited architectural transparency.

data_layer.rationale: DynamoDB + S3, daily backups, multi-AZ, per-tenant data segregation, data classification and retention policies in place. No caching layer or data warehouse mentioned.

security_posture.rationale: MFA on admin/remote access and SCM, quarterly access reviews, 1-day access revocation, firewall + IDS, monthly vuln scanning, annual pen test, endpoint protection, AWS KMS encryption. No WAF explicitly mentioned. Cybersecurity insurance held.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC confirmed, branch protection with required approvals, separate environments, emergency change process, monthly patch management. CI/CD tooling and source control platform not named — limits visibility.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, reviewed and tested annually per multiple scenarios, daily backups, multi-AZ. No RTO/RPO stated. No multi-region failover.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Only AWS explicitly named as infrastructure provider. No other cloud vendors, CDN, monitoring tools, or identity providers named. High single-vendor concentration on AWS.

overall.rationale: Solid foundational SOC 2 Type 2 posture for a small company. Clean audit with 0 exceptions. Key gaps: no multi-region DR, no WAF, limited vendor diversity disclosed, tools unnamed (SCM, CI/CD, monitoring). Financial AI document automation with appropriate financial sector controls in place.

red_flags: No multi-region failover — single region deployment creates geographic concentration risk
No WAF mentioned — financial data platform without explicit WAF coverage
RTO/RPO not disclosed despite BCDR policy and testing
yellow_flags: CI/CD tooling and source control platform not named — limits vendor risk assessment
Monitoring/alerting tool not named — cannot assess tool quality
Single cloud provider (AWS) with no secondary provider or CDN
Small org structure (CEO/CTO + COO + Dev Team) creates key-person dependency risk
No SSO mentioned for customer-facing authentication
green_flags: SOC 2 Type 2 with 0 exceptions over 8-month observation period
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
MFA enforced for admin access, remote access, and source code management
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
BCDR plan tested annually with multiple loss scenarios
Board has Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) meeting semiannually
key_observations: Bicameral is a financial document AI company (factoring/accounts receivable) — financial data sensitivity warrants stricter scrutiny of data segregation controls
The CEO also serves as CTO — technical leadership concentration at a single individual
Trust criteria explicitly excludes Processing Integrity and Privacy — unusual for a financial data processor; worth investigating scope rationale
4 untestable controls due to no events during the period (no incidents, no terminations) — not exceptions, but untested operating effectiveness in those scenarios
Baseline configs as code with rollback capability is a strong IaC maturity signal for a small company
Product suite (ExamFlow, Aging Extraction, Factoring Portfolio Audit) suggests multi-product architecture that may not be fully reflected in the single VPC diagram shown
